---
title: Moist Energy Constraints on Surface Temperature Variance Under Climate Warming
pagetitle: 2025-5-1-Ge2025b - Gang Chen
body-classes: publication-page
format:
  html:
    title-block-style: none
year: 2025
author: Bowen Ge, Gang Chen, Jian Lu and Wenyu Zhou
venue: Journal of Advances in Modeling Earth Systems
venue_cit: <i>Journal of Advances in Modeling Earth Systems</i>, 17, doi:10.1029/2024MS004612.
recommended_citation: 'Bowen Ge, Gang Chen, Jian Lu and Wenyu Zhou, 2025: Moist Energy
  Constraints on Surface Temperature Variance Under Climate Warming, <i>Journal of
  Advances in Modeling Earth Systems</i>, 17, doi:10.1029/2024MS004612.'
paper_url: https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1029/2024MS004612
abstract: Understanding the factors controlling surface temperature variance is crucial
  for predicting temperature extremes. Previous investigations have examined individual
  impacts of temperature advection and surface turbulent fluxes on temperature fluctuations.
  Here, we explore the constraints on temperature variance from the moist static energy
  (MSE) balance and introduce a new scaling relation that connects the generation
  of temperature variance through moist energy transport with its dissipation due
  to the net energetic forcing of the atmosphere. This theory is evaluated in an idealized
  aquaplanet model. We find that surface temperature variance is influenced by eddy
  (sensible) heat flux, MSE gradient, and the Clausius‐Clapeyron relation for evaporative
  cooling. Under global warming, the reduced temperature variance in the aquaplanet
  model is dominated by the weakening in eddy heat flux, but it is also affected by
  changes in evaporative cooling and MSE gradient, which may be more important in
  realistic, moisture‐limited regions over land.
---

::: {.page-with-sidebar}

::: {.sidebar-col}
{{< include /_author-profile.qmd >}}
:::

::: {.content-col}

# Moist Energy Constraints on Surface Temperature Variance Under Climate Warming

*Published in Journal of Advances in Modeling Earth Systems, 2025*

**Recommended citation:** Bowen Ge, Gang Chen, Jian Lu and Wenyu Zhou, 2025: Moist Energy Constraints on Surface Temperature Variance Under Climate Warming, <i>Journal of Advances in Modeling Earth Systems</i>, 17, doi:10.1029/2024MS004612.

## Abstract

Understanding the factors controlling surface temperature variance is crucial for predicting temperature extremes. Previous investigations have examined individual impacts of temperature advection and surface turbulent fluxes on temperature fluctuations. Here, we explore the constraints on temperature variance from the moist static energy (MSE) balance and introduce a new scaling relation that connects the generation of temperature variance through moist energy transport with its dissipation due to the net energetic forcing of the atmosphere. This theory is evaluated in an idealized aquaplanet model. We find that surface temperature variance is influenced by eddy (sensible) heat flux, MSE gradient, and the Clausius‐Clapeyron relation for evaporative cooling. Under global warming, the reduced temperature variance in the aquaplanet model is dominated by the weakening in eddy heat flux, but it is also affected by changes in evaporative cooling and MSE gradient, which may be more important in realistic, moisture‐limited regions over land.

[Download paper](https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1029/2024MS004612){target="_blank"}

:::

:::
